reasoning: As of November 2025, there are approximately 50 days left until the January 1, 2026 deadline. The search results summarizing recent information up to the knowledge cutoff reveal no evidence of the Chinese government or the US intelligence community publicly announcing an established or confirmed Chinese military base in any BRICS country (such as the UAE, Egypt, Iran, Russia, South Africa, etc.). Setting up and officially announcing a military base is a long process that takes years, and we only see evidence of China 'pursuing potential bases' (e.g., in the UAE or Egypt) as of earlier intelligence reports, with no formal announcements or firm confirmations having materialized over 2024 or 2025. Furthermore, any establishment of such a structural footprint would generate intense media and intelligence scrutiny long before completion. Given the extremely tight time horizon of less than two months and the absence of any developments signaling an imminent announcement, it is highly unlikely this will occur before 2026. Therefore, a floor probability of 5% is assigned.
batch_queries: Will China operate at least one military base in a BRICS country before 2026?
